beginner.question: What is a Schottky diode?
beginner.answer: A metal-semiconductor diode with low forward drop and very fast switching.

beginner.question: Why is it fast?
beginner.answer: No minority-carrier storage, so no reverse-recovery time.

intermediate.question: Where use a Schottky?
intermediate.answer: Low-voltage, high-frequency rectification and freewheeling where low V_F matters.

intermediate.question: Main drawback vs p-n?
intermediate.answer: Lower reverse voltage rating and higher leakage, especially when hot.

advanced.question: Why does leakage matter?
advanced.answer: At high temperature Schottky leakage can rival load current, wasting power and heating.

expert.question: How choose between Schottky and synchronous rectification?
expert.answer: Schottky for simplicity/low cost; synchronous Metal–Oxide–Semiconductor Field-Effect Transistor (MOSFET) rectification for highest efficiency at very low V_out.
